jokes.text: Maybe we'll see Natalie Morales, you know? That little firecracker, she gets my blood running south of the border.
jokes.setup: Dennis mentions seeing Natalie Morales
jokes.punchline: His crude sexual euphemism about blood running 'south of the border'
jokes.explanation: Establishes Dennis as crude and inappropriate. Character-specific humor that fits his lowbrow personality.

jokes.text: Aw, you said 'Chinese' instead of something offensive.
jokes.setup: Dennis mentions getting Chinese food
jokes.punchline: Liz's relief that he used a non-offensive term
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Liz's low expectations for Dennis. Economical and reveals their relationship dynamic perfectly.

jokes.text: Yes, I'm annoying, not the man who honked your boobs on the jumbo screen.
jokes.setup: Someone judges Liz for being back with Dennis
jokes.punchline: Her defensive response about the boob honking incident
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing Liz's defensiveness and Dennis's past behavior. Very quotable and specific.

jokes.text: That was before. And it was the playoffs.
jokes.setup: Boob honking on jumbo screen
jokes.punchline: Liz justifying it because it was the playoffs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that perfectly captures Liz's character. The idea that playoffs justify public boob honking is hilariously illogical.

jokes.text: The old Beeper King retired... Well, technically he shot himself.
jokes.setup: Dennis is now the Beeper King
jokes.punchline: The previous king's dark fate
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy escalation. The casual delivery of such dark information is quintessential 30 Rock.

jokes.text: You sent a letter to Scott Peterson. After he dyed his hair and got super thin from all the stress.
jokes.setup: Jenna defending herself against dating advice
jokes.punchline: Her attraction to a convicted murderer during his trial stress
jokes.explanation: Incredibly dark and character-specific. Jenna's shallow vanity finding a murderer attractive because of his stress weight loss is perfectly twisted.

jokes.text: Fast and only on Saturdays. It's perfect.
jokes.setup: Question about sex with Dennis
jokes.punchline: Her low standards and satisfaction with minimal intimacy
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Liz's low expectations and workaholic priorities. The enthusiasm for terrible sex is perfectly Liz.

jokes.text: Oh...'Normal.' How dare they?
jokes.setup: Tracy showing an article calling him 'normal'
jokes.punchline: Liz's deadpan reaction to his outrage at being called normal
jokes.explanation: Solid reaction beat. Liz's sarcastic reading shows her exasperation with Tracy's ego.

jokes.text: It only looks like I'm walking out of a Starbucks when, actually, I'm doing the robot goin' backwards into a Starbucks.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining a paparazzi photo
jokes.punchline: His absurd excuse for appearing normal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - creating an elaborate backstory to avoid appearing normal. The specificity makes it brilliant.

jokes.text: If I'm not a movie star, then I'm poor. And poor people can't afford to pay back the $75,000 in cash they owe Quincy Jones!
jokes.setup: Tracy's chain of logic about being normal
jokes.punchline: The specific debt to Quincy Jones
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with a wonderfully specific and random detail about owing Quincy Jones money.

jokes.text: What's a drive-in? Of course. I don't know why I bothered to ask.
jokes.setup: Testing Jenna's age with cultural references
jokes.punchline: Her not knowing what a drive-in is confirms her youth
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Jack's investigative skills and generational gaps. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I can tell just from your physical appearance that you're obviously... 29.
jokes.setup: Jack building up to reveal Jenna's age
jokes.punchline: The pause before confirming her lie
jokes.explanation: Well-structured pause comedy. Jack's diplomatic handling of Jenna's obvious lie.

jokes.text: Yeah, like if she walked in right now and said she wanted to do it with you, you would. I definitely would.
jokes.setup: Others teasing Josh about loving Liz Taylor
jokes.punchline: His immediate, sincere confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Josh's earnest enthusiasm and lack of embarrassment about his crush.

jokes.text: When someone asks an actress how old she is, it's more statement than question.
jokes.setup: Jenna's distress about age question
jokes.punchline: Philosophical observation about ageism in Hollywood
jokes.explanation: Smart observation delivered in character - good blend of wit and character truth

jokes.text: But she's old. She's like 38. I'm gonna be sick.
jokes.setup: Discussing Jenna's mother getting work done
jokes.punchline: Jenna's horror at the 'old' age of 38
jokes.explanation: Solid character comedy about Hollywood age standards. Jenna's vanity and age panic perfectly displayed.

jokes.text: What tragedy happened in your life that you insist upon punishing yourself with all this... mediocrity?
jokes.setup: Jack observing Liz's lifestyle choices
jokes.punchline: His dramatic diagnosis of her mediocrity as self-punishment
jokes.explanation: Good Jack dialogue showing his pompous but insightful nature. The word 'mediocrity' delivered with such disdain.

jokes.text: you have a piece of lettuce stuck in your hair.
jokes.setup: Jack criticizing Liz's lifestyle
jokes.punchline: The visual confirmation of his point
jokes.explanation: Solid visual gag that proves Jack's point about Liz's presentation. Sets up callback later.

jokes.text: He gave us the names of the most discreet private investigators to spy on our ex-wives.
jokes.setup: Jack praising Jack Welch's mentorship
jokes.punchline: Escalates from nice perks to surveillance of ex-wives
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jack's warped view of corporate culture

jokes.text: There appears to be a gentleman making passionate, angry, love to himself.
jokes.setup: Jack looking out the window
jokes.punchline: His euphemistic description of someone masturbating
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack delivery with his tendency toward pretentious euphemisms. 'Passionate, angry love' is wonderfully overwrought.

jokes.text: Am I wrong, or is he in the middle of a staff meeting?
jokes.setup: Continuing to observe the masturbating man
jokes.punchline: The revelation that it's happening during a meeting
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of an already absurd situation. The professional context makes it even more outrageous.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, I hope it's his daughter. Yeah, me too. Oh, my God! Oh! God, I hope it's his mistress.
jokes.setup: Playing mistress or daughter about an older man with a young woman
jokes.punchline: Their rapid reversal when they see more details
jokes.explanation: Good escalation comedy. The quick reversal of their preferences based on visual evidence.

jokes.text: Hey, 007, how about some more rolls over here, huh?
jokes.setup: Dennis addressing the waiter at an upscale restaurant
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate James Bond reference
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dennis's inappropriate behavior in upscale settings. Establishes his class insecurity.

jokes.text: I didn't know they served chicken nuggets at this restaurant. It's cod. It's, uh-- They made it special.
jokes.setup: Jack observing Dennis's food choice
jokes.punchline: Liz defending Dennis eating kid food at fancy restaurant
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Liz's embarrassment and Dennis's inappropriateness

jokes.text: Actually, I'm allergic to all fish unless it's fried.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining why he's eating chicken nuggets at an upscale restaurant
jokes.punchline: His absurd fish allergy logic
jokes.explanation: Great Dennis logic - the idea that frying somehow neutralizes a fish allergy is perfectly stupid and character-specific.

jokes.text: You know there are 17 rats per person in Manhattan? You eat a pound of rat crap every year without even knowing it, huh?
jokes.setup: Dennis making dinner conversation at an upscale restaurant
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate rat facts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis behavior - completely inappropriate dinner conversation that shows his lack of social awareness.

jokes.text: I hope you enjoy the, uh, choices that you've made.
jokes.setup: Jack leaving Liz and Dennis at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive goodbye about life choices
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crafted passive-aggressive Jack line - extremely quotable and character-specific

jokes.text: And that former call girl went on to become one of NBC's biggest news anchors.
jokes.setup: Jack telling an anecdote to his date
jokes.punchline: The casual revelation about an NBC news anchor
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with meta NBC reference. The casual way Jack drops this bombshell is perfect.

jokes.text: [screaming] No!
jokes.setup: Tracy encountering people at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Inexplicable screaming reaction
jokes.explanation: Random Tracy moment - in character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: He fixed my toilet. He broke it first, but he fixed it.
jokes.setup: Liz defending Dennis's niceness
jokes.punchline: The revelation that he caused the problem he fixed
jokes.explanation: Perfect structure showing Liz's relationship pattern. The quick reversal reveals how low her standards are.

jokes.text: I can't just go to Central Park and join the singles touch football league. Like I'd want to anyway. People in those posters, always laughing in their sweaters. Who are they foolin'?
jokes.setup: Liz explaining why she can't date normally
jokes.punchline: Her specific rant about the people in dating activity posters
jokes.explanation: Excellent Liz character comedy. Her specific cynicism about dating advertisement imagery is perfectly neurotic and relatable.

jokes.text: Dennis is my boyfriend because he inquired. He was the only applicant, and I am not-- Doin' great.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to justify her relationship choice
jokes.punchline: Her breakdown mid-sentence admitting she's not doing well
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment. The job application metaphor and her mid-sentence breakdown perfectly capture Liz's romantic desperation.

jokes.text: Really? This water-- does someone boil it first, then throw it in your face?
jokes.setup: Jenna claiming her plastic surgery results are just from water and rest
jokes.punchline: Liz's sarcastic description of Jenna's obviously altered appearance
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy description - creative and quotable insult

jokes.text: No, younger even. You look like a fetus.
jokes.setup: Jenna asking if she looks ten years younger after procedures
jokes.punchline: Liz's brutal assessment
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual comedy payoff. 'Fetus' is perfectly insulting and economical. Shows the extreme results of Jenna's procedures.

jokes.text: I just mentioned Dennis, and your eyebrows didn't go up. They didn't? No. How about now? Unh-uh. Anything?
jokes.setup: Liz testing if Jenna's Botox worked
jokes.punchline: Jenna trying and failing to move her eyebrows
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy showing the consequences of Jenna's procedures. Calls back to the earlier eyebrow reactions.

jokes.text: If anyone sees a salamander, it's Liz's. No. I don't want a salamander!
jokes.setup: Dennis brought Liz a present
jokes.punchline: The salamander escaping and Liz's rejection
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Dennis's inappropriate gift choices and Liz's exasperation.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, for now. But the beeper's gonna be making a comeback. Technology's cyclical.
jokes.setup: Liz saying people need cell phones, not beepers
jokes.punchline: Dennis's delusional business philosophy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis delusion. 'Technology's cyclical' as justification for selling obsolete beepers is brilliantly stupid.

jokes.text: it's when a bunch of rats are crammed into a tiny space, and their tails get all tangled up, and they can't even pull apart. And then it gets awesome. Eventually, their bodies fuse together and they form a multi-headed live rat king.
jokes.setup: Explaining what a rat king is
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic description and 'And then it gets awesome'
jokes.explanation: Great Dennis moment. His excitement about something so gross is perfectly in character. Sets up the metaphor for later.

jokes.text: [whispering] I love him.
jokes.setup: Dennis's oblivious responses
jokes.punchline: Jack's whispered confession of affection for Dennis's stupidity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment. His appreciation for Dennis as entertainment shows his sophisticated sense of irony.

jokes.text: Who normal now? You hear me, America? Who's normal now?
jokes.setup: Tracy showing off his fake tattoo
jokes.punchline: His declaration to America about not being normal
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Tracy's earlier concern about normalcy. His dramatic address to America is appropriately over-the-top.

jokes.text: And when you purchase Tracy Jordan, he comes with a tattoo of a biblical dragon from outer space.
jokes.setup: Tracy defending his tattoo choice
jokes.punchline: Describing himself as a purchasable product with ridiculous tattoo description
jokes.explanation: Great Tracy logic - treating himself as commodity with absurd fantasy description

jokes.text: Jenna looks like a porn star burn victim, and now this idiot.
jokes.setup: Liz dealing with cast appearance problems
jokes.punchline: Her brutal description of Jenna's post-surgery look
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual description. 'Porn star burn victim' is hilariously specific and harsh, perfect for Liz's exasperation.

jokes.text: I'm here to give you the gift... of pain. The gift of what? White diamonds! Ha ha!
jokes.setup: The dramatic buildup about giving Josh a gift
jokes.punchline: The perfume spray and revelation it's about White Diamonds perfume
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection comedy. The dramatic buildup to a perfume commercial reference is well-structured.

jokes.text: I'm here to give you the gift... of pain. The gift of what? White diamonds!
jokes.setup: Fake Liz Taylor offering Josh 'the gift of pain'
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's an attack followed by perfume ad reference
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and payoff for Josh's celebrity crush setup

jokes.text: Oh, I can't. I'm expecting a call from 1983.
jokes.setup: Jack asking Liz to take off the beeper Dennis sold her
jokes.punchline: Her sarcastic comment about the beeper's obsolescence
jokes.explanation: Excellent one-liner about beeper obsolescence. The specific year makes it funnier and shows Liz's wit.

jokes.text: I'm married with two beautiful kids and a pool. (Jack) Liz, I wasn't trying to set you up. I wanted to show you my handiwork.
jokes.setup: Liz assuming Jack is trying to set her up
jokes.punchline: Howard being married and Jack's real intention
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Jack's actual mentoring agenda and Liz's defensive assumptions.

jokes.text: I dressed poorly, had bad posture, walked around with lettuce in my hair. Oh, son of a bitch.
jokes.setup: Howard describing his past similarities to Liz
jokes.punchline: Liz realizing she still has lettuce in her hair
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure. The lettuce returning as proof of Jack's point about Liz's habits.

jokes.text: Now Howard's earning seven figures and he's married to a swell Filipino gal.
jokes.setup: Jack describing Howard's success
jokes.punchline: Casual somewhat offensive characterization of Howard's wife
jokes.explanation: Character comedy showing Jack's casual insensitivity

jokes.text: Are we still talking about the sandwich place? No, sadly, I don't think we are.
jokes.setup: Liz describing the sandwich place in terms that apply to Dennis
jokes.punchline: The recognition that she's really talking about her relationship
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor structure. The parallel between settling for a mediocre sandwich place and Dennis is perfectly drawn.

jokes.text: Are we still talking about the sandwich place? No, sadly, I don't think we are.
jokes.setup: Liz ranting about sandwich place
jokes.punchline: Acknowledgment that she's really talking about Dennis
jokes.explanation: Good meta-moment acknowledging the metaphor - well-structured revelation

jokes.text: Dennis isn't a sandwich I want to eat every day for the rest of my life.
jokes.setup: Continuing the sandwich metaphor
jokes.punchline: The explicit comparison of Dennis to an unappetizing sandwich
jokes.explanation: Good extension of the metaphor. The phrasing makes Dennis sound unappetizing in multiple ways.

jokes.text: because sadly, you may be the most stable person I know right now.
jokes.setup: Liz asking Jack for life advice
jokes.punchline: Admitting Jack is her most stable friend
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how messed up Liz's life is

jokes.text: Because he wears shirts with the Looney Tunes embroidered on them. Because he cuts his own hair.
jokes.setup: Liz listing reasons to break up with Dennis
jokes.punchline: The specific and pathetic details about his appearance
jokes.explanation: Good character specificity. These details paint a vivid picture of Dennis's lack of sophistication.

jokes.text: That's right. He's the rat king.
jokes.setup: Liz realizing her entanglement with Dennis
jokes.punchline: Jack connecting to Dennis's earlier rat king story as metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that transforms absurd rat story into perfect relationship metaphor

jokes.text: That's right. He's the rat king. And there's only one way to break up with a rat. You have to cut him off completely.
jokes.setup: Liz describing getting tangled up with Dennis
jokes.punchline: Jack connecting it to Dennis's earlier rat king description
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback and metaphor. Taking Dennis's own gross rat description and applying it to their relationship is perfectly structured comedy.

jokes.text: You have to stuff your heart with steel wool and tinfoil. You must be ruthless. You must to be absolute. Remember always, you are The Exterminator.
jokes.setup: Jack giving breakup advice using the rat metaphor
jokes.punchline: His dramatic, over-the-top coaching with 'The Exterminator'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Jack's dramatic mentoring style. 'The Exterminator' title is appropriately overwrought.

jokes.text: Look, I'm crazy, not stupid. A movie star can't have a big old permanent face tattoo.
jokes.setup: Revealing the tattoo is fake
jokes.punchline: Tracy's logical explanation for why it has to be fake
jokes.explanation: Good character logic. Tracy's distinction between crazy and stupid shows his self-awareness about his image.

jokes.text: I had extreme plastic surgery to have it removed. Ba-boom! That's another 'Not Normal.'
jokes.setup: Question about what happens when the fake tattoo is gone
jokes.punchline: Tracy's plan for another publicity stunt
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Tracy's image management strategy. His planning ahead for the next stunt is perfectly calculated.

jokes.text: Wayne Brady has three Emmys. You have a People's Choice Award that you stole from Wayne Brady.
jokes.setup: Tracy comparing himself to Wayne Brady
jokes.punchline: The revelation that he stole Wayne Brady's award
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation comedy. The specific detail about stealing Wayne Brady's People's Choice Award is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: First of all, I'm not from Whiteville. I'm from White Haven, and it's not as nice as it sounds.
jokes.setup: Tracy's comment about Liz being from Whiteville
jokes.punchline: Her correction and the implication that White Haven is somehow worse
jokes.explanation: Good character background comedy. The idea that a place called White Haven could be not as nice as it sounds is perfectly Liz.

jokes.text: [indistinctly] And I'm John Kerry.
jokes.setup: Dress rehearsal with cast in bad shape
jokes.punchline: Unintelligible impression during live performance
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing production disaster - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: No wonder your career is being held back by a lack of foresight and an addiction to dysfunctional relationships.
jokes.setup: Liz having no contingency plan for the show disaster
jokes.punchline: Jack's brutal analysis connecting it to her personal life
jokes.explanation: Good Jack analysis connecting professional and personal failings. His psychological insight delivered as professional criticism.

jokes.text: He knows about my marriage? No.
jokes.setup: Jack mentioning addiction to dysfunctional relationships
jokes.punchline: Liz's paranoia about Jack knowing personal details
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's paranoia about privacy

jokes.text: Yo, this blackout messed me up. I didn't get to debut my dragon face. Could I do the Today Show tomorrow?
jokes.setup: Building-wide blackout saves bad show
jokes.punchline: Tracy only concerned about missing his fake tattoo debut
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy self-centeredness in crisis with callback to dragon tattoo

jokes.text: Tattoo's fake, Donaghy. Fake. Street cred. He's a genius.
jokes.setup: Jack offering tattoo removal
jokes.punchline: Tracy revealing it's fake and Jack appreciating the strategy
jokes.explanation: Good callback resolution with Jack's appreciation for Tracy's scheme

jokes.text: There's no reason to live anymore. What happened? The Islanders lost tonight. Doesn't that happen a lot?
jokes.setup: Dennis's dramatic despair
jokes.punchline: The trivial reason and Liz's practical observation
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Dennis's misplaced priorities and Liz's exasperation with his dramatics.

jokes.text: So how did it go? He moved in with me. Well, of course he did.
jokes.setup: Jack asking about the breakup
jokes.punchline: The complete opposite result and Jack's unsurprised reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending showing Liz's complete failure and Jack's resignation to her poor choices. Great reversal of expectations.
